revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hokumon Shrine]][[ja:北門神社]]


{{Infobox religious building
| name = {{nihongo|'''Hokumon Jinja'''|北門神社}}
| image = File:稚内 北門神社 - panoramio.jpg
| caption = 
| map_type = Japan
| map_alt = 
| coordinates = {{Coord|45.420017|N|141.671314|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| map_relief  = 1
| map_size = 
| map_caption = 
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| type = 
| deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}}
| founded_by = {{ill|Q22127716|qid=Q22127716|1=Murayama Denbee}}
| established = {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 (1785)
| date_destroyed = 
| location = 1-1-21 Chūō, [[:en:Wakkanai, Hokkaido|Wakkanai]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}
| website = 
| architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}
| festival = 5 July
| leadership = 
| location = {{ill|Wakkanai|qid=Q267894|1=Wakkanai}}
}}

{{Nihongo|'''Hokumon Jinja'''|北門神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in [[:en:Wakkanai, Hokkaido|Wakkanai]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], Japan. In {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 (1785), [[Matsumae Domain]] trader and agent {{nihongo|[[Murayama Denbee]]|[[:ja:村山伝兵衛|村山伝兵衛]]}} (1738–1813) is said to have founded the small shrine of {{nihongo|Sōya Daijingū|宗谷大神宮}}, enshrining [[Amaterasu]] as guardian of the north gate.<ref name="Jinjacho">{{cite web |url=https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/北門神社/ |script-title=ja:北門神社 |trans-title=Hokumon Jinja |language=Japanese |publisher=Hokkaidō Jinja-chō |accessdate=28 September 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.city.wakkanai.hokkaido.jp/gaiyo/ayumi.html |script-title=ja:稚内のあゆみ（歴史）|trans-title=History of Wakkanai |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Wakkanai, Hokkaido|Wakkanai City]] |accessdate=28 September 2019}}</ref> In 1896 the shrine was transferred to its current location and renamed Hokumon Jinja, with [[Takemikazuchi]] and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] enshrined alongside Amaterasu. In 1902 work on the shrine buildings was completed, but on 17 May 1911 the whole complex was destroyed by a wild fire. The year 1913 saw the rebuilding of the [[honden]] and [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]] and in 1916 Hokuman Jinja was [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Ranked]] as a Village Shrine. In 1925 the shrine offices were donated and in 1933 Hokumon Jinja was promoted to the Rank of District Shrine. The hexagonal shrine [[mikoshi]] was dedicated in 1949 and in 1978 the new shrine building was completed and a transfer ceremony held.<ref name="Jinjacho"/>

==See also==
* {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}}
* [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
